items.title: 'Destroys souls': Coercive control legislation tabled in ACT but laws still years away

children.content: The government's bill does not detail specific coercive control behaviours which it says is deliberate.

gallery.altText: A woman talking in her office.

thumbnail.altText: A woman talking in her office.

items.title: Victim of suspicious unit fire in Canberra identified

children.content: ACT Policing is treating the death of Kylee McHugh, 46, as a homicide and calling for people to come forward if they had recently seen her.

image.altText: A women takes a selfie.

items.title: Wheel falls off Link Airways flight during take-off from Canberra Airport

children.content: Damage to bearing parts meant investigators could not determine what caused a wheel to separate from a Link Airways-operated flight departing Canberra for Newcastle.

image.altText: A plane's wheels, with one wheel missing.

items.title: Controversial CIT CEO gives emphatic denial of EV training misconduct allegations

children.content: The CEO of the Canberra Institute of Technology has strongly denied allegations that student mechanics worked on Hertz hire cars without the company's knowledge, insisting the claims are "factually incorrect" and "offensive" to staff and students.

gallery.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

thumbnail.altText: A professional in front of CIT signage

items.title: Canberra man becomes first person in ACT charged with exit trafficking

children.content: The man, who left his wife in India and did not return for the birth of his son, is the first person in the ACT to be charged with the crime.

image.altText: A de-identified man walks between two police officers.

items.title: Man avoids jail after indecently abusing woman he met on Tinder

children.content: The ACT Supreme Court heard the man was concerned only with his own sexual gratification when he abused his victim.

image.altText: A Tinder screen.

items.title: Accused 'money mules' refused bail after allegedly moving millions from scams

children.content: Police say so-called "money mules" facilitate the swift movement of fraudulently obtained money.

image.altText: Cash

items.title: Man discovers Stolen Generations truth after decades believing he was adopted

children.content: Ricky Hampson spent most of his life believing he had been adopted before learning several years ago he is a survivor of the Stolen Generations. Today, he is calling for more action and truth-telling.

image.altText: A man looks serious in a shirt with the slogan "Always Was".

items.title: ACT public school system to be unified in 'generational shift'

children.content: The ACT's public education system will be significantly reformed after a review of resourcing found schools operated much like "independent small businesses".

gallery.altText: A group of primary school students sit in a classroom facing away.

thumbnail.altText: A group of primary school students sit in a classroom facing away.

items.title: Government sues 3M Australia for $2 billion over PFAS contamination

children.content: The Commonwealth is launching its largest-ever legal claim, suing the manufacturer of a firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals that contaminated 28 Defence Force bases across Australia.

gallery.altText: Fire fighter sprays foam on a fire

thumbnail.altText: Fire fighter sprays foam on a fire

items.title: ABC appoints Reuters exec Simon Robinson as news director

children.content: Senior Reuters executive Simon Robinson takes over from former director Justin Stevens who announced his resignation yesterday after four years in the job.

image.altText: A man with greyish hair poses for a photo.

items.title: Greens signal willingness to help Labor push capital gains changes through in weeks

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers introduced the bill to parliament today, which the government hopes to pass before the winter recess.

image.altText: The treasurer glances at the camera to his right as he sits in parliaments among other frontbenchers.

items.title: Nationals pull election stunt as tax debate heats up

children.content: This week a few people got a grilling at Senate estimates, the tax debate heated up and whispers of a new centre-right party fluttered through the press gallery.

image.altText: Man standing in front of Australian flag with hands in prayer

items.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers have WA quarantine period extended

children.content: Six people who were on a cruise ship that was hit by an outbreak of hantavirus have had their quarantine period in the outer Perth suburb of Bullsbrook extended until June 23.

image.altText: The exterior of a quarantine facility on the outskirts of Perth.

items.title: Meet the Iranians who gather every night outside Khamenei's house

children.content: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.

gallery.altText: A woman dressed in traditional clothing mourns outside on a street.
A crowd of women mourning outside a house.
A mural of Ali Khamenei on the street of Tehran.

items.title: No new evidence in Gus Lamont search, but police 'won't give up'

children.content: A renewed search for missing four-year-old boy Gus Lamont has failed to uncover additional evidence, police reveal.

image.altText: An SA Police officer at Oak Park Station.

items.title: Woman charged with joining IS remanded in custody

children.content: Police conducted searches at homes in Melbourne's north early on Thursday as part of ongoing investigations into people who have returned from Syria.

image.altText: A woman in black Islamic garb is flanked by two uniformed police officers.

items.title: Greens push for minister's resignation after legal fee revelation

children.content: Madeleine Ogilvie is being accused of deliberately misleading parliament, after she revealed her involvement in a Supreme Court matter, months after saying she was not connected to any legal action.

image.altText: A woman sits in a green chair in parliament

items.title: Lawyer says Joshua Brown will admit to childcare centre sex crimes

children.content: Joshua Brown, who has been charged with 156 offences, including sexual assault, has appeared in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court via video link.

image.altText: A man smiles in a photo with coworkers

items.title: The surprising city claiming to be Australia's street art capital

children.content: Artists say Darwin has surpassed Melbourne as Australia's street art capital
with work underway on a fresh round of murals across the Top End city.

items.title: Four Corners reporter Mahmood Fazal sacked by ABC

children.content: Mahmood Fazal has been sacked by the ABC following an internal investigation into an appearance on a podcast hosted by a former underworld figure, managing director Hugh Marks says.

image.altText: A man with a full black beard.

items.title: Young finds method in the madness for long-awaited Origin arrival

children.content: It took three years and four series, but when Hudson Young was given the chance to impose his unorthodox abilities on State of Origin football, he didn't hesitate to harness the chaos for New South Wales.

image.altText: Hudson Young celebrates scoring a try in an Origin match

children.content: Small changes to the way we approach our meal planning can make a big difference to the overall cost.

children.content: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

children.content: From Hildegard von Bingen to Hildur Guðnadóttir, here are some of the most influential women of the music world in history and today.

children.content: Queensland Corrective Services say the drone was carrying illicit drugs, syringes and a mobile phone.

items.title: Family 'burst into tears' after Lauren Huxley attacker denied parole

children.content: Robert Black Farmer was handed a 24-year sentence over the attempted murder of Lauren Huxley at her Northmead home in 2005.

image.altText: Sydney TAFE student Lauren Huxley

items.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate in coming decades

children.content: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.

image.altText: The third will collected by the Office of the Public Trustee Queensland.

items.title: Man arrested over alleged 1994 armed robbery at Melbourne shopping centre

children.content: A man who allegedly carried out an armed robbery and shot three men at Chadstone Shopping Centre in 1994 has been arrested by police in Queensland.

image.altText: The exterior of Chadstone Shopping Centre with a Christmas wreath hanging as decoration on it.

items.title: Alleged murderer's bail in limbo as Tiwi Islands town erupts in riots

children.content: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.

image.altText: A photo from a plane shows a remote community sitting on the edge of an island, part of the aircraft's wing is visible.

items.title: Supercars ref sorry for calling driver 'a grub' over controversial crash

children.content: The Supercars referee offers an apology to a driver after describing him as "a bit of a grub" because of his actions at the conclusion of last year's championship.

gallery.altText: Racing driver in the garage, holding up his index finger

thumbnail.altText: Racing driver in the garage, holding up his index finger

items.title: IS-linked mother back in childhood bedroom in Sydney's south-west

children.content: The father of one of the returned Islamic State group-linked women says his daughter is "happy to be home" but exhausted after more than a decade stranded in Syria.

image.altText: Nesrine Zahab with her veil lifted

items.title: Bolivian president warns country at 'breaking point' after weeks of protests

children.content: The protests have caused shortages of food, fuel and life-saving medicine and members of Bolivia's Indigenous community have called for the president's resignation.

image.altText: A small fire in front of several police officers on motorbikes or standing.

items.title: Housing investors are increasingly older and wealthier, Reserve Bank research shows

children.content: Reserve Bank research shows property investment has become skewed towards higher-income households and is dominated by baby boomers.

image.altText: a skyline with houses
